_comment: Connector-specific edits to GENERATED/authored files that a cli-printing-press reprint must NOT clobber. check_handfixes.py asserts every marker below is still present (and banned anti-patterns absent) and fails CI if a reprint dropped one. Provenance: project_pagerduty_onboard_traps.md (PR #48, 2026-06-06, press 4.22.0).

handfixes.summary: guide.md (vendored from the PagerDuty README) had ~96 ../../api-reference and ~9 openapiv3 deep-links into a non-shipped tree; repointed to developer.pagerduty.com and stripped to code text
handfixes.why: PagerDuty's source README carried ~96 [API Concepts](../../api-reference/<hash>...) links into a tree msp-skills doesn't ship plus ~9 [/path](openapiv3.json/...) OpenAPI spec deep-links - check_md_links FAILs. Fixed: repoint api-concepts links to the real vendor docs (https://developer.pagerduty.com/api-reference/, verified 200; check_md_links SKIPS http(s) so a 404 would slip) and strip openapiv3 spec deep-links to backtick code text. A reprint re-vendors the broken relative/openapiv3 links.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Fix belongs in the press README-vendoring step: rewrite or strip relative ../../ and openapiv3 deep-links that don't resolve in the shipped tree. Grep guide.md for `](../../` and `](openapiv3` after vendoring any new spec.

handfixes.summary: Analytics differentiator framed as offline / without the paid Analytics tier / no per-question call - NOT 'no API returns it' (which is false; the binary wraps the vendor Analytics API)
handfixes.why: Round-1 adversarial P1: README/SKILL/guide/page.json claimed MTTR-by-service / off-hours-pages were 'questions no single PagerDuty API call returns' - contradicted by the binary's own analytics commands that wrap the vendor Analytics API. The honest, defensible claim is 'offline / without the paid Analytics add-on / no live call per question'. A reprint of the authored marketing prose could reintroduce the false absolute claim.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Not press-encodable - a prose/marketing review rule. The fresh-context adversarial reviewer (not the gates) is the only thing that catches it; refines feedback_outcome_prose_must_match_command_output_fields.

handfixes.summary: Top-level manifest.json exists (press 4.22.0 emitted only tools-manifest.json; recovered from the prior scaffold commit so build-catalog stops failing)
handfixes.why: Press run 20260606-085517 emitted only tools-manifest.json, not the mcpb/DXT manifest.json onboard copies in step 3 - build-catalog.py failed 'missing manifest.json for skill'. Recovered from the prior partial-scaffold commit. A reprint from a press that still drops manifest.json reintroduces the missing-file failure; this ledger entry makes its absence a loud failure.
